title: Quantitative Susceptibility Mapping (QSM)
sub_domain: Quantitative MR susceptibility recovery (multi-physics joint inverse)
source_data_quality_note: v3 standalone multi-physics genesis Principle. Joint forward operator couples MR phase encoding (Larmor / Bloch) with Maxwell magnetostatic dipole-field perturbation; recovers tissue magnetic susceptibility chi(r) from measured GRE phase. Distinct from L1-059 SWI (phase-weighted display only) by sensing-mechanism primitives and by solution_space (quantitative susceptibility map vs phase-weighted image). Authored under the standalone-as-default v3 design (see pwm-team/pwm_product/genesis/PWM_V3_STANDALONE_VS_COMPOSITE.md).

E.description: Quantitative Susceptibility Mapping (QSM): joint multi-physics forward couples gradient-echo MR phase encoding (Larmor precession under static + perturbed field) with Maxwell magnetostatic dipole-field perturbation arising from spatially varying tissue magnetic susceptibility chi(r). The forward DAG has 7 primitives with two coupling constraints (n_c = 2): (i) phase-to-field linear coupling phi = gamma * TE * (B_z - B_0); (ii) field-to-susceptibility dipole convolution (B_z - B_0) = D_kernel(*) chi where D_kernel(k) = (1/3) - k_z^2/|k|^2 is the Lorentzian dipole response in k-space. Recovery is posed as the joint linear inverse problem that recovers chi(r) from measured complex GRE signal across multiple TE. Difficulty tier delta = 5 with raw condition number kappa ~ 240 (zero-cone singularity at the magic angle ~ 54.7 degrees) and effective kappa_eff ~ 30 after wave-cone regularization. Mismatch parameters: B0_inhomogeneity, brain_segmentation_error, phase_unwrap_error, dipole_kernel_truncation_error, background_field_removal_error. Additive Gaussian thermal/electronic noise sets the data-fidelity floor. See forward_model field for the closed-form joint imaging equation.
E.forward_model: phi(r) = gamma * TE * B_z(r); B_z(r) - B_0 = D_kernel(*) chi(r); jointly invert phi(r) -> chi(r) via dipole-convolution operator (k-space wave-cone regularization or model-based MEDI / TGV-QSM)
E.world_state_x: 3D tissue magnetic susceptibility chi(r) [ppm] — diamagnetic for most tissue, paramagnetic for iron-rich regions (substantia nigra, globus pallidus, microbleeds), positive for calcifications and contrast agents
E.observation_y: Multi-echo gradient-echo (GRE) complex MR signal at declared TE; magnitude and phase across N_echoes

G.dag: L.rf_excitation -> L.gradient_echo -> L.larmor_precession -> L.maxwell_magnetostatic -> L.dipole_convolution -> L.phase_extract -> int.spatial
coupling_constraints: phase_to_field: phi = gamma * TE * (B_z - B_0) — analytic linear coupling, well-conditioned
field_to_susceptibility: B_z - B_0 = D_kernel(*) chi — dipole-convolution coupling, ill-conditioned at magic angle (kappa raw ~ 240)

W.regime: Existence of recovered 3D quantitative susceptibility chi(r) is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the wave-cone-supported subspace; modes inside the magic-angle zero cone are recovered only through priors (MEDI L1-edge, TGV second-order, deep-prior). Stability is moderately conditioned (kappa_eff ~ 30 after wave-cone regularization) — B0_inhomogeneity and background_field_removal_error dominate the stability cliff; phase_unwrap_error and dipole_kernel_truncation contribute higher-order bias terms. Joint Hadamard well-posedness for the coupled phase-field-susceptibility forward is established by Liu, de Rochefort, Wharton, Bilgic literature 2009-2018 (see references).

C.solver_class: linear-operator + convex optimisation [MEDI, TKD, iLSQR, TGV-QSM] | linear-operator + deep neural prior [QSMnet, xQSM]
C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: ||chi_hat - chi||_2 <= C1 * sigma_noise / sqrt(N_measurements * N_echoes). Wave-cone-regularization-induced bias adds + C2 * |zero_cone_mode_energy| + C3 * |B0_inhomogeneity| + C4 * ||chi - chi_s||_1 (best s-sparse / piecewise-smooth approximation residual). MEDI / TGV-QSM iterative solvers achieve q=2 (O(1/k^2)) near the MLE under L1-edge or TGV prior; deep-prior variants achieve faster initial convergence with bounded systematic bias. epsilon_fn captures monotone quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(H * W * Z * log(H * W * Z)) per iteration via FFT-based dipole-convolution forward / adjoint; learned variants O(H W Z * F_theta_cost) per forward pass

joint_well_posedness_references: Liu C et al. (2009) Calculation of susceptibility through multiple orientation sampling (COSMOS). Magn Reson Med 61(1):196-204.
de Rochefort L et al. (2010) Quantitative susceptibility map reconstruction from MR phase data using Bayesian regularization (MEDI). Magn Reson Med 63(1):194-206.
Wharton S, Bowtell R (2010) Whole-brain susceptibility mapping at high field. NeuroImage 53(2):515-525.
Liu T et al. (2012) Morphology enabled dipole inversion (MEDI) from a single-angle acquisition. Magn Reson Med 66(3):777-783.
Bilgic B et al. (2014) Fast quantitative susceptibility mapping with L1-regularization and automatic parameter selection. Magn Reson Med 72(5):1444-1459.
Langkammer C et al. (2018) Quantitative susceptibility mapping: report from the 2016 reconstruction challenge. Magn Reson Med 79(3):1661-1673.

distinctness_audit.rationale: L1-059 SWI uses sensing_mechanism = mri_fourier_encoding with primitives {L.rf_excitation, L.gradient_echo, L.phase_mask, int.spatial} and solution_space = 3D_susceptibility (phase-weighted display). L1-503 QSM uses sensing_mechanism = mri_gre_phase_with_maxwell_magnetostatic with primitives {L.rf_excitation, L.gradient_echo, L.larmor_precession, L.maxwell_magnetostatic, L.dipole_convolution, L.phase_extract, int.spatial} and solution_space = 3D_quantitative_susceptibility. Jaccard distance over (sensing_mechanism, solution_space, primitives) ~ 0.5 — solidly Distinct under v1 advisory thresholds (>= 0.30).

v3_metadata.clinical_context: Routine clinical imaging at academic medical centers. Sensitive to iron deposition (Parkinson's, Alzheimer's, MS), calcification (tumors, vascular), demyelination (MS lesions), brain microbleeds, deep-brain-stimulation electrode targeting. Typical scan time 5-8 min at 3T. Reconstruction time 30 s - 5 min depending on solver.
